docs.title: Upsurge
docs.text: Welcome to the Upsurge documentation! Upsurge is a Python library for creating multi-format reports with support for sections, text, images, plots, tables, and multiple pages with internal links.

docs.title: What is Upsurge?
docs.text:
Upsurge provides a simple, powerful way to generate reports:

  • Multi-page Reports: Create reports with multiple pages and navigation
  • Rich Content: Support for text, images, matplotlib plots, and pandas tables
  • Multiple Formats: Export to Markdown, HTML, and PDF
  • Internal Linking: Link between pages within your report

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Type-safe - Built with Python type hints
  • Extensible - Add custom content elements and styling
  • Multiple Export Formats - Markdown, HTML, and PDF support
  • Automatic Table of Contents - Generated for multi-page reports

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
from upsurge import Report, Page, Text, Section
from upsurge.publishers import HTMLPublisher

report = Report(
    title="My Report",
    author="John Doe",
    description="A sample report"
)

page = Page(id="introduction", title="Introduction")
page.add_content(Text(content="Welcome to my report!"))

section = Section(title="Background", level=2)
section.add_content(Text(content="Some background information..."))
page.add_section(section)

report.add_page(page)

publisher = HTMLPublisher()
publisher.publish(report, "my_report.html")

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Getting Started - Install and set up Upsurge
  • Quick Start - Build your first report
  • API Reference - Complete API documentation

docs.title: License
docs.text: Upsurge is released under the MIT License.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page documents the public API for Upsurge.

docs.title: Report
docs.text:
The main container for a report with multiple pages.

@dataclass
class Report:
    title: str = "Untitled Report"
    author: Optional[str] = None
    description: Optional[str] = None
    pages: list[Page] = field(default_factory=list)

Methods:

  • add_page(page: Page) -> Report - Add a page to the report
  • get_page(page_id: str) -> Optional[Page] - Get a page by ID
  • to_markdown() -> str - Convert report to Markdown string
  • to_html() -> str - Convert report to HTML string
  • save_markdown(path: str) -> None - Save report as Markdown file
  • save_html(path: str) -> None - Save report as HTML file
  • save_pdf(path: str) -> None - Save report as PDF file

docs.title: Page
docs.text:
A single page in a report.

@dataclass
class Page:
    id: str = ""
    title: str = ""
    content: list[ContentElement] = field(default_factory=list)
    sections: list[Section] = field(default_factory=list)
    toc_entry: bool = True

Methods:

  • add_content(element: ContentElement) -> Page - Add content to the page
  • add_section(section: Section) -> Page - Add a section to the page
  • to_markdown() -> str - Convert page to Markdown
  • to_html() -> str - Convert page to HTML

docs.title: Section
docs.text:
A section within a page for organizing content.

@dataclass
class Section:
    title: str = ""
    level: int = 1
    id: Optional[str] = None
    content: list[ContentElement] = field(default_factory=list)
    subsections: list[Section] = field(default_factory=list)

Methods:

  • add_content(element: ContentElement) -> Section - Add content to the section
  • add_subsection(section: Section) -> Section - Add a subsection
  • to_markdown(base_level: int = 1) -> str - Convert section to Markdown
  • to_html() -> str - Convert section to HTML
  • find_by_id(section_id: str) -> Optional[Section] - Find a section by ID

docs.title: Content Elements
docs.text: All content elements inherit from ContentElement and implement to_markdown() and to_html() methods.

docs.title: Text
docs.text:
Plain text with optional styling.

@dataclass
class Text(ContentElement):
    content: str = ""
    format: str = "plain"
    style: Optional[str] = None  # "bold", "italic", "code"

docs.title: Image
docs.text:
An image from a file path or URL.

@dataclass
class Image(ContentElement):
    path: Optional[str] = None
    url: Optional[str] = None
    alt: str = ""
    width: Optional[str] = None
    height: Optional[str] = None
    caption: Optional[str] = None

docs.title: Plot
docs.text:
A matplotlib figure embedded in the report.

@dataclass
class Plot(ContentElement):
    figure: Any = None
    caption: Optional[str] = None
    format: str = "png"
    width: Optional[int] = None
    height: Optional[int] = None

docs.title: Table
docs.text:
A table with headers and data rows.

@dataclass
class Table(ContentElement):
    data: list[list[Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
    headers: Optional[list[str]] = None
    caption: Optional[str] = None
    column_align: Optional[list[str]] = None  # "left", "center", "right"

docs.title: Link
docs.text:
An external hyperlink.

@dataclass
class Link(ContentElement):
    text: str = ""
    url: str = ""
    new_tab: bool = False

docs.title: PageLink
docs.text:
An internal link to another page in the report.

@dataclass
class PageLink(ContentElement):
    text: str = ""
    page_id: str = ""
    page_title: Optional[str] = None

docs.title: Publishers
docs.text: Publishers convert a Report to a specific format.

docs.title: MarkdownPublisher
docs.text:
class MarkdownPublisher:
    def publish(self, report: Report, path: str) -> None

docs.title: HTMLPublisher
docs.text:
class HTMLPublisher:
    def publish(self, report: Report, path: str) -> None

docs.title: PDFPublisher
docs.text:
class PDFPublisher:
    def publish(self, report: Report, path: str) -> None

docs.title: Contributing to Upsurge
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to Upsurge!

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/kwierman/upsurge.git
cd upsurge

  2. Install dependencies using uv: 

    uv sync

  3. Install development dependencies: 

    uv sync --group dev

docs.title: Running Tests
docs.text:
Run the test suite:

uv run pytest

Run tests with coverage:

uv run pytest --cov=src/upsurge --cov-report=html

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
This project uses Ruff for linting:

uv run ruff check src/

Format code:

uv run ruff format src/

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
upsurge/
├── src/upsurge/           # Main package
│   ├── __init__.py        # Public API and Report class
│   ├── content/           # Content elements
│   │   ├── base.py        # ContentElement base class
│   │   ├── page.py        # Page class
│   │   └── section.py     # Section class
│   └── publishers/       # Output publishers
│       ├── markdown.py    # MarkdownPublisher
│       ├── html.py        # HTMLPublisher
│       └── pdf.py         # PDFPublisher
├── tests/                 # Test suite
├── docs/                  # Documentation
└── examples/             # Example usage

docs.title: Building Documentation
docs.text: uv run mkdocs serve

docs.title: Submitting Changes
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch (git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature)
  3. Commit your changes (git commit -m 'Add amazing feature')
  4. Push to the branch (git push origin feature/amazing-feature)
  5. Open a Pull Request

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text: Please report issues on the GitHub issue tracker.

docs.title: License
docs.text: By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the MIT License.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or higher
  • pip or uv package manager

docs.title: Install with pip
docs.text: pip install upsurge

docs.title: Install with uv
docs.text: uv add upsurge

docs.title: Install for Development
docs.text: pip install upsurge[dev]

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
After installation, verify that Upsurge is installed correctly:

python -c "import upsurge; print(upsurge.__version__)"

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
Upsurge depends on:

  • markdown-it-py (>=3.0.0) - For Markdown parsing
  • jinja2 (>=3.1.0) - For templating
  • weasyprint (>=60.0) - For PDF generation
  • matplotlib (>=3.7.0) - For plot rendering
  • pandas (>=2.0.0) - For table handling

These are installed automatically with Upsurge.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Proceed to the Quick Start guide to learn how to use Upsurge.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide will walk you through the basics of using Upsurge to create reports.

docs.title: Step 1: Create a Report
docs.text:
Create a Python file (e.g., report.py) and create a Report instance:

from upsurge import Report, Page, Text
from upsurge.publishers import HTMLPublisher

report = Report(
    title="My Report",
    author="John Doe",
    description="A sample report"
)

docs.title: Step 2: Add Pages
docs.text:
Create pages and add content to them:

page = Page(id="introduction", title="Introduction")
page.add_content(Text(content="Welcome to my report!"))

report.add_page(page)

docs.title: Step 3: Add Sections
docs.text:
Organize content into sections:

from upsurge import Section

page2 = Page(id="details", title="Details")

section = Section(title="Background", level=2)
section.add_content(Text(content="Some background information..."))
page2.add_section(section)

report.add_page(page2)

docs.title: Step 4: Export the Report
docs.text:
Export to your desired format:

# HTML
publisher = HTMLPublisher()
publisher.publish(report, "report.html")

# Markdown
from upsurge import MarkdownPublisher
md_publisher = MarkdownPublisher()
md_publisher.publish(report, "report.md")

# PDF
from upsurge import PDFPublisher
pdf_publisher = PDFPublisher()
pdf_publisher.publish(report, "report.pdf")

docs.title: Working with Tables
docs.text:
import pandas as pd
from upsurge import Table

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Name": ["Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"],
    "Age": [25, 30, 35],
})

table = Table(
    data=df.values.tolist(),
    headers=list(df.columns),
    caption="Employee List"
)

page.add_content(table)

docs.title: Working with Plots
docs.text:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from upsurge import Plot

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot([1, 2, 3], [1, 4, 9])

plot = Plot(
    figure=fig,
    caption="A quadratic curve"
)

page.add_content(plot)

docs.title: Adding Links
docs.text:
from upsurge import Link, PageLink

# External link
page.add_content(Link(text="Visit Google", url="https://google.com", new_tab=True))

# Internal link to another page
page.add_content(PageLink(text="Go to Details", page_id="details"))

docs.title: Adding Images
docs.text:
from upsurge import Image

page.add_content(Image(path="image.png", caption="A sample image"))

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Explore the API Reference for complete documentation
  • Check out the examples in the examples/ directory for more use cases
